mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals expanding labor income and consumption demand, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward returns following month-over-month payroll increases are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 7.0%, elevated labor slack signals weak household income growth and dampened corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of months when UNRATE > 7.0% over a 3-year rolling window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: When the 3-month annualized change in CPIAUCSL exceeds the federal funds rate, negative real policy rates drive capital reallocation into equities, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: 3-month annualized CPIAUCSL change exceeds FEDFUNDS level
failure_condition: If the joint signal does not produce positive 20-day returns in at least 60% of triggered instances over any 2-year rolling window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: In the early stage of a post-recession recovery, month-over-month gains in housing starts (HOUST) signal durable demand revival and construction sector momentum, lifting S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If 60-day forward returns are not significantly higher than zero (t-test p > 0.05) during triggered post-recession windows over a 5-year sample, reject.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in industrial production (INDPRO) signals improving manufacturing profitability and capacity utilization, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If positive 5-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of triggered months over a 3-year rolling window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate below 0.25% reflects maximum monetary accommodation that suppresses short-term discount rates and supports risk assets, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns during FEDFUNDS < 0.25% periods are not significantly positive versus baseline (t-test p > 0.05) over any 2-year window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) growth above 2% quarter-over-quarter signals robust aggregate demand that supports corporate revenue expansion and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) when GDPC1 growth exceeds 2% over a 3-year rolling window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous weakness in industrial production (INDPRO) and rising unemployment (UNRATE) signals a broadening economic contraction that erodes earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases month-over-month while INDPRO decreases month-over-month
failure_condition: If the joint signal fails to produce negative 20-day returns in at least 60% of triggered instances over any 2-year rolling window, reject.
